name: Thai Green Curry Chicken with Basil
recipeIngredient: 3 cans unsweetened coconut milk (do not shake)
2 whole chicken breasts, boned, skinned, cut into 1-inch cubes
8 dried or fresh kaffir lime leaves or fresh citrus leaves
3/4 cup basil leaves (Thai basil or standard green basil)
2 cups fresh green peas or 8 small Thai eggplants
Chicken stock or water, as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Skim about 1 cup of thick coconut cream from the top of the coconut milk and discard.
2. In a wok or saucepan, combine the remaining coconut milk, galangal, and fish sauce. Cook over high heat until the mixture becomes thick and oily.
3. Add green curry paste and cook until aromatic, about 2 minutes.
4. Add chicken pieces and cook over medium heat for about 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Stir in the remaining coconut milk, citrus leaves, half of the basil, chilies, and peas. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Adjust the sauce thickness with chicken stock or water if necessary.
7. Garnish with the remaining basil leaves and serve hot over rice.
